g.text: 3. PICKUP IS REMOVED BEFORE SPIKE DETECTION: 50/100/150 Hz

text.style: font-size: 9.5px; font-family: 'DejaVu Sans', 'Bitstream Vera Sans', 'Computer Modern Sans Serif', 'Lucida Grande', 'Verdana', 'Geneva', 'Lucid', 'Arial', 'Helvetica', 'Avant Garde', sans-serif

g.text: are in the LFP band, cut by the ~300 Hz spike high-pass

path.style: fill: none; stroke: #4c78a8; stroke-width: 1.4; stroke-linecap: square

font-size: 10.5px; font-family: 'DejaVu Sans', 'Bitstream Vera Sans', 'Computer Modern Sans Serif', 'Lucida Grande', 'Verdana', 'Geneva', 'Lucid', 'Arial', 'Helvetica', 'Avant Garde', sans-serif

g.text: THE CONTAMINATION — three independent 'tells' that the LFP 50 Hz is electrical PICKUP, not neural:

text.style: font-size: 10.5px; font-family: 'DejaVu Sans', 'Bitstream Vera Sans', 'Computer Modern Sans Serif', 'Lucida Grande', 'Verdana', 'Geneva', 'Lucid', 'Arial', 'Helvetica', 'Avant Garde', sans-serif

g.text: (1) it scales with drive & appears on DEAD electrodes, (2) it sits ON the dead electrodes spatially, (3) but it lives below the spike band, so it is removed before spike detection.
